summary: Decompiled the Spy Cloak item source by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itspycloak.s with src/melee/it/items/itspycloak.c, marking the object Matching, routing obj_files.mk to build the C object, localizing most internal symbols, and cleaning the header/prototypes. The final version resolved the initial nonmatching concern around the zero float by using a literal 0 assignment instead of an extern sdata2 float, and fixed the ItemStateTable length by reading the original assembly table rather than trusting objdiff's unresolved unlinked .o view.
what_changed: Deleted the old 258-line assembly implementation at asm/melee/it/items/itspycloak.s.
Added src/melee/it/items/itspycloak.c implementing the Spy Cloak item functions it_80295C48 through it_80295EB4 and the ItemStateTable it_803F6370.
Changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itspycloak.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itspycloak.c").
Changed obj_files.mk so the build uses $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itspycloak.c.o instead of the old asm/melee/it/items/itspycloak.s.o.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to mark many helper functions in the it_80295C68..it_80295E80 range as local instead of global.
Changed the zero float symbol at .sdata2:0x804DCB70 from global it_804DCB70 to local @170, matching the final C source's use of literal 0 instead of an extern const float.
Added a declaration for it_80276CB8(Item_GObj* gobj) to src/melee/it/it_2725.h.
Simplified src/melee/it/items/itspycloak.h by using it/types.h, adding address comments to public prototypes, and keeping only exported functions plus extern ItemStateTable it_803F6370.
smart_moves: Converted internal item callbacks to static functions in the C file and reflected that in symbols.txt as local scope, avoiding unnecessary global exports.
Used the existing item helper APIs and common item patterns: GET_ITEM, Item_80268E5C, it_8026B390, it_8026D62C, it_80272860, it_8026E15C, it_8026DA08, it_8026E8C4, and it_8026B894.
Reconstructed it_803F6370 as five ItemStateTable entries matching the assembly table layout instead of padding a guessed single-entry table with zeros.
Removed unnecessary casts from Item_GObj* to HSD_GObj* in Item_80268E5C calls once review clarified they were only an m2c quirk.
Avoided the initial .sdata2 alignment issue for it_804DCB70 by emitting velocity zeroing as literal 0 assignments, allowing the local float symbol to remain local and eliminating the explicit extern.
decomp_lessons: Do not blindly trust objdiff's apparent table size or unresolved function pointers when comparing an unlinked .o; inspect the original assembly data table directly when reconstructing ItemStateTable arrays.

For item state tables, a flat .4byte sequence in assembly can correspond to multiple ItemStateTable struct initializers, commonly groups of four fields such as { flags_or_zero, anim_cb, phys_cb, coll_cb }.
m2c may introduce casts that are not semantically required in the hand-written C; reviewer feedback confirmed Item_GObj* casts to HSD_GObj* were unnecessary here.

If a local float constant creates alignment or symbol-scope friction, check whether the source can use a literal constant that emits the desired local sdata2 entry instead of declaring a global extern.
When a function is only referenced inside the translated file or its state table, prefer static C definitions and local symbol scope.
After moving an asm item to C, update both configure.py and obj_files.mk so the build system stops compiling the asm object and classifies the C object correctly.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based symbol names such as it_80295C48, it_80295D90, and it_803F6370 because no semantic names were available in the evidence.
Used Item_GObj* gobj as the consistent parameter spelling in the final C implementation.
Header prototypes include address comments in the style /* 295C48 */ void it_80295C48(Item_GObj*); and /* 3F6370 */ extern ItemStateTable it_803F6370[].
Local anonymous sdata2 float was represented in symbols.txt as @170 rather than exported as it_804DCB70.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched the assembly's zeroing of x40_vel.x/y/z by assigning item->x40_vel.x = item->x40_vel.y = item->x40_vel.z = 0.
Mapped simple return stubs from assembly into bool callbacks returning false and empty void callbacks.
Matched repeated fall-physics code by loading ItemAttr from item->xCC_item_attr and passing attr->x10_fall_speed and attr->x14_fall_speed_max to it_80272860.
Matched state-change callbacks by passing function pointers such as it_80295D04 and it_80295C68 into item helpers it_8026D62C, it_8026E15C, and it_8026E8C4.

Rebuilt the .data state table from the original .4byte list as five ItemStateTable entries: {0,it_80295CB8,it_80295CC0,it_80295CC4}, {0,it_80295D2C,it_80295D34,it_80295D64}, {0,it_80295DB8,NULL,NULL}, {0,it_80295D2C,it_80295DE8,it_80295E18}, and {0,it_80295E74,it_80295E7C,it_80295E80}.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: The casts from Item_GObj* to HSD_GObj* were described as an m2c setup quirk and not necessary.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested trimming the initial source/split issue in splits.txt.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Pointed out that asm/melee/it/items/itspycloak.s showed it_803F6370 was longer than the initial guessed table; objdiff likely did not resolve function pointers because the .o was unlinked.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Referenced follow-up changes in commit 74b584c1b2e68c1f47d842059f35391b6d12b518 for the contributor to study.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename itspycloak functions from address-based names to semantic Spy Cloak names.
Search for other item PRs where ItemStateTable reconstruction required reading the original .s data instead of relying on objdiff.
Search for other fixes involving local sdata2 float symbols such as @170 and alignment differences.
Check commit 74b584c1b2e68c1f47d842059f35391b6d12b518 for the exact post-review changes referenced by ribbanya.
